summary: date-fns-lite postinstall exfiltrates cloud, SSH, and git credentials to hardcoded remote host
details: This package impersonates the popular date-fns library while shipping a malicious postinstall.js that runs automatically on `npm install`. The postinstall script harvests installer-side secrets — AWS credentials/config, Azure accessTokens.json, gcloud application_default_credentials.json, kubeconfig, SSH private keys (id_rsa, id_ed25519, id_ecdsa) and known_hosts,.gitconfig/.git-credentials, ~/.npmrc, and Docker config.json — and, when running as root inside a container, pivots through /proc/1/root to read host /etc/passwd and /etc/shadow and queries AWS IMDSv2 at 169.254.169.254 to steal IAM role credentials. Collected data is POSTed as JSON to http://115.190.124.243:9082/callback. The script also branches on specific hostnames (DESKTOP-9RU5G9J, ubuntu-fc-uvm, scan-*) to select environment-specific collection routines, indicating targeted operation and sandbox evasion. index.js exposes only a trivial formatDate/timeAgo shim as cover.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
